title: hymenolepis incisa
intro: Hymenolepis incisa, also known as Hairy Woodpecker Flower, is a flowering plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Hymenolepis incisa include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as a treatment for wounds and skin infections. Herbal preparations of Hymenolepis incisa often involve infusions of the leaves and flowers, which can be consumed as teas or used topically as poultices.
